message:
feat(parser,cli): expand Mermaid parser with class diagram and note support, extend CLI config validation

The Mermaid parser gains class diagram member parsing and note/comment
attachment support. The CLI config system is extended with additional
validated options and round-trip test coverage. The evidence binary
and wasm bindings receive minor refinements.

- fm-parser/mermaid_parser.rs: add class diagram member parsing
  (attributes, methods, visibility modifiers); add note attachment
  to sequence diagrams; expand parser error recovery paths
- fm-parser/ir_builder.rs: extend IR builder with class member and
  note node types
- fm-parser/lib.rs: adjust parser exports and mode handling
- fm-cli/main.rs: extend config validation with additional runtime
  checks; expand render option plumbing
- fm-cli/tests/config_roundtrip_test.rs: expand config round-trip
  coverage with new validated options
- fm-cli/tests/integration_test.rs: adjust integration test assertions
- fm-cli/bin/evidence.rs: refine evidence binary output
- fm-render-term/diff.rs: adjust diff rendering
- fm-wasm/src/lib.rs: minor wasm binding adjustment
- tests/sequence_fragment_test.rs: adjust fragment test
- Update README and beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-b0pc.16","title":"Add fm-cli interactive command to README Command Reference","description":"Gap found during refinement pass 2: The 'fm-cli interactive' command is fully implemented (split-pane TUI editor with live preview, theme selection, Ctrl+Q/Ctrl+S, crossterm-based) but is NOT listed in the README Command Reference section. README documents render, parse, detect, validate, capabilities, diff, watch, serve — but skips interactive.

## Scope
1. Add a new subsection to README under Command Reference:
   ### fm-cli interactive
   Launch a split-pane terminal editor with live diagram preview.
   ```bash
   fm-cli interactive input.mmd
   fm-cli interactive input.mmd --theme dark
   ```
   Requires a terminal (not available in CI/pipes).
2. Mention the interactive command in the Quick Start section as an alternative workflow
3. Note keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+Q to quit, Ctrl+S to save
4. Mention it requires terminal detection (isatty check)

## Verification
- grep 'interactive' README.md should find the new section
- The documented flags (--theme, --parse-mode) match the actual clap definition

## Cross-reference
- Should be done after bd-b0pc.2 (both edit README command reference area)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:36:20.912059201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:30:01.870212999Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T04:30:01.869929923Z","close_reason":"Added fm-cli interactive command to README: new Command Reference subsection with usage examples and keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Q/Ctrl+S), plus Quick Start step 6 mentioning interactive editing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","docs","readme","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.16","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:36:20.912059201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.16","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:37:23.934922098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.17","title":"Add comprehensive tests for fm-cli diff command","description":"Gap found during refinement pass 2: The fm-cli diff command is fully implemented with 4 output formats (summary, plain, terminal, json) but has ZERO dedicated tests anywhere in the test suite.

## Test Cases
1. Basic diff: two flowcharts where one node label changed
   - Input A: 'flowchart LR; A[Hello]-->B'
   - Input B: 'flowchart LR; A[World]-->B'
   - Assert: diff output mentions A label changed, B unchanged

2. Node addition diff:
   - Input A: 'flowchart LR; A-->B'
   - Input B: 'flowchart LR; A-->B-->C'
   - Assert: diff reports C as added node, new edge B-->C

3. Edge removal diff:
   - Input A: 'flowchart LR; A-->B; B-->C'
   - Input B: 'flowchart LR; A-->B'
   - Assert: diff reports edge B-->C removed, node C removed

4. Identical diagrams:
   - Same input for both A and B
   - Assert: diff reports no changes (or empty diff)

5. All 4 output formats:
   - Run each test case with --format summary, plain, terminal, json
   - Assert: summary format contains change counts
   - Assert: json format is valid JSON with added/removed/changed arrays
   - Assert: terminal format contains ANSI color codes (when terminal detected)
   - Assert: plain format has no ANSI codes

6. Cross-type diff:
   - Input A: 'flowchart LR; A-->B' (flowchart)
   - Input B: 'stateDiagram-v2; [*]-->Active' (state)
   - Assert: diff handles gracefully (different types = everything changed)

## Implementation
- Add as fm-cli/tests/diff_test.rs or extend integration_test.rs
- Use assert_cmd or direct binary invocation
- Log all output for debugging

## Verification
- cargo test -p fm-cli -- diff (at least 6 test functions pass)
- Each output format produces non-empty, correctly structured output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:36:37.936951865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:44:19.405474515Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T04:44:19.405266743Z","close_reason":"Added 8 end-to-end diff tests using parsed Mermaid input: label change, node addition, edge removal, identical diagrams, summary/plain/terminal output formats, cross-diagram-type diffing. All 22 diff tests pass (14 existing + 8 new).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","diff","reality-check","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.17","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:36:37.936951865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.18","title":"Assess pie and xyChart rendering completeness for README status upgrade","description":"Gap found during refinement pass 2: Both pie and xyChart have dedicated SVG rendering functions (render_pie_svg at lib.rs:2861, render_xychart_svg at lib.rs:3075), yet README marks both as 'Partial'. The parser is complete for both types. Need to assess whether the rendering is good enough to upgrade status.

## Scope
1. Pie chart assessment:
   - Render: 'pie title Pets; Dogs: 70; Cats: 20; Birds: 10'
   - Check: Are wedges rendered with correct proportions? Are labels visible? Does showData work?
   - Check: Do different themes produce different pie colors?
   - If gaps: list specific missing features (legends, custom colors, percentage labels)
   - Decision: upgrade to 'Implemented' or document what's missing

2. XyChart assessment:
   - Render: 'xychart-beta; title Sales; x-axis [Q1,Q2,Q3,Q4]; line [10,20,15,25]'
   - Check: Are axes rendered with labels? Are data points plotted? Do bars render?
   - Render: multi-series with line + bar combination
   - Check: Does area fill work? Are axis ticks present?
   - If gaps: list specific missing features (stacked bars, annotations, thresholds)
   - Decision: upgrade to 'Implemented' or document what's missing

3. For whichever types are upgraded: update the supported-diagram-types table in README

## Verification
- Golden SVG test for pie chart with 3+ slices
- Golden SVG test for xyChart with line and bar series
- Visual inspection of output (are the charts readable and correctly proportioned?)

## Cross-reference
- Related to bd-b0pc.1 (edits same README table)
- If both upgrade to Implemented, the project has 22 Implemented + 1 Partial (sequence) + 1 Partial (if sequence stays partial)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:36:55.646404545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:55:32.736584176Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T04:55:32.736282234Z","close_reason":"Assessed pie and xychart rendering: both are feature-complete for static SVG. Pie renders wedges, radial labels, legend, title, percentages. XyChart renders axes, gridlines, ticks, bar/line/area series, legend, title. Added 4 quality tests confirming rendering. Upgraded both from 'Partial' to 'Implemented' in README. Now 23/24 diagram types Implemented.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pie","reality-check","render","svg","xychart"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.18","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:36:55.646404545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.18","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:37:24.046962739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-b0pc.19","title":"Make remaining frankenmermaid.toml parser/core knobs truly runtime-configurable","description":"Runtime config loading for fm-cli is now wired, but several documented knobs are still validation-only rather than behaviorally applied. Current state: core.deterministic=false, core.fallback_on_error=false, parser.intent_inference=false, parser.fuzzy_keyword_distance != 2, parser.auto_close_delimiters=false, and parser.create_placeholder_nodes=false fail fast with clear errors because the parser/detector pipeline still bakes those semantics in. Scope: thread these settings into detection/parser recovery behavior (or revise docs/schema if some should be removed), add focused unit/integration coverage, and prove behavior via CLI tests rather than load-time validation only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:36:53.975304989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:36:53.975304989Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","config","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.19","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:36:53.975304989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-b0pc.19","title":"Make remaining frankenmermaid.toml parser/core knobs truly runtime-configurable","description":"Runtime config loading for fm-cli is now wired, but several documented knobs are still validation-only rather than behaviorally applied. Current state: core.deterministic=false, core.fallback_on_error=false, parser.intent_inference=false, parser.fuzzy_keyword_distance != 2, parser.auto_close_delimiters=false, and parser.create_placeholder_nodes=false fail fast with clear errors because the parser/detector pipeline still bakes those semantics in. Scope: thread these settings into detection/parser recovery behavior (or revise docs/schema if some should be removed), add focused unit/integration coverage, and prove behavior via CLI tests rather than load-time validation only.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:36:53.975304989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:21:26.900667543Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:21:26.900373576Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","config","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.19","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:36:53.975304989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.2","title":"Update README layout algorithm count from 10 to 16 and list all algorithms","description":"Reality check found: README claims '10 Layout Algorithms' but LayoutAlgorithm enum has 16 variants (excluding Auto): Sugiyama, Force, Tree, Radial, Timeline, Gantt, XyChart, Sankey, Kanban, Grid, Sequence, Pie, Quadrant, GitGraph, Packet + a constraint-programming solver (good_lp integration).

## Scope
1. Update '10 Layout Algorithms' to '16 Layout Algorithms' in the Why Use feature table
2. Update the Algorithm Auto-Selection table in 'How the Layout Engine Works' to list ALL 16 algorithms (currently only shows 10)
3. Add missing algorithms to the table: Pie, Quadrant, GitGraph, XyChart, Packet, Grid
4. Update the TL;DR section if it mentions '10+'
5. Search for any other '10 layout' or '10 algorithm' references in README.md and AGENTS.md
6. Verify LayoutAlgorithm::as_str() entries match what's documented

## Verification
- Count algorithm rows in the Auto-Selection table — should be 16
- grep -c '10 Layout\|10 algorithm' README.md AGENTS.md should return 0
- Each LayoutAlgorithm variant appears in at least one doc table

## Cross-reference
- Depends on bd-b0pc.1 (edits same README file — do sequentially)
- Related to bd-6qd.1 (README writing)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:37:55.688388400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:27:28.041131552Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:27:28.040862312Z","close_reason":"Updated README layout algorithm counts and auto-selection documentation to match the live LayoutAlgorithm enum: 15 algorithms excluding Auto, with all algorithm ids now represented in the docs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","readme","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.2","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:37:55.688388400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.2","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T23:04:17.306430558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.3","title":"Fix npm package name: README says @frankenmermaid/core but pkg is fm-wasm","description":"Reality check found: README says 'npm install @frankenmermaid/core' and all TypeScript import examples use '@frankenmermaid/core', but pkg/package.json has name 'fm-wasm'.

## Decision
Rename the package to '@frankenmermaid/core' to match the README branding. This is the user-facing name and must be consistent.

## Scope
1. Update pkg/package.json: change name from 'fm-wasm' to '@frankenmermaid/core'
2. Update build-wasm.sh if it sets or references the package name
3. Check if wasm-pack generates the name field — if so, update Cargo.toml [package] metadata or the wasm-pack --out-name flag
4. Update any internal imports that reference 'fm-wasm' as a package name
5. Verify the package.json has correct metadata: description, repository, homepage, keywords, license fields
6. Ensure the package has a proper README in the npm package (copy or symlink main README)

## Verification
- cat pkg/package.json | jq .name should output '@frankenmermaid/core'
- re-run build-wasm.sh and verify the generated package.json still has the correct name
- The TypeScript definition file imports should work with the new name

## Cross-reference
- bd-b0pc.6 (TypeScript defs) depends on this — do this first
- Related to bd-6qd.2 (NPM package distribution, already closed) — this is a naming fix","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:37:55.816766474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:41:14.239368480Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:41:14.239089281Z","close_reason":"Fixed the WASM npm package branding to @frankenmermaid/core, made build-wasm.sh regenerate package metadata and pkg/README.md deterministically, and repaired wasm32 build blockers; remaining gzip-budget failure is tracked separately in bd-kugr.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","npm","reality-check","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:37:55.816766474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.4","title":"Verify and fix install.sh curl installer end-to-end","description":"Reality check found: README prominently features 'curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash' as the primary install method, but this has never been verified end-to-end.

## Scope
1. Read install.sh and document what it does (download pre-built binary? build from source? check platform?)
2. Test in a clean environment: run the install script and verify fm-cli is available afterward
3. Fix any issues found: broken URLs, missing platform detection, incorrect binary names
4. Verify the installed binary passes a smoke test: 'echo flowchart LR; A-->B | fm-cli detect -' should output 'Flowchart'
5. Add a CI job (GitHub Actions) that runs the installer in a clean ubuntu container and verifies the binary works
6. If the installer downloads from GitHub Releases, verify releases exist and contain the correct binaries

## Test Plan
- Run install.sh in a docker ubuntu:latest container
- Verify fm-cli is in PATH after install
- Run: fm-cli detect, fm-cli render --format svg, fm-cli capabilities
- Verify non-zero exit code on genuinely bad input
- Log all output with timestamps for debugging

## Cross-reference
- Related to bd-6qd.3 (CLI binary distribution via GitHub Releases)
- Related to bd-6qd.5 (release canary workflow) — the installer test IS a canary","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.117219987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:53:05.281108873Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distribution","install","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.4","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.117219987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -397,7 +397,7 @@ Example `frankenmermaid.toml`:
 ```toml
 # Global behavior
 [core]
-deterministic = true          # Enforce deterministic output
+deterministic = true          # Reserved: output remains deterministic regardless
 max_input_bytes = 5_000_000   # Input size limit
 fallback_on_error = true      # Best-effort on parse failure
